title: 20 years after alleged crime, child abuse  trial finally begins
summary: It took her 10 years to tell what happened, and another 10 for the case to go to trial, but the woman, now 31, says she remembers it all. Even in a system where the time between charges and verdict can be years, the Sean Wright child sex abuse trial is unusual. "This is surreal," the woman said Monday as she prepared to walk into an Anchorage courtroom and, for the first time in more than a decade, see the man police say molested her starting when she was 9 years old. Even after all these years, she still feels uncomfortable talking about it and doesn't want her full name used. Her first name is Nellie. Back then, Nellie was a gawky kid who says she woke up early every morning to make sure her alcoholic mother got to work on time. Today she is a polished attorney from Boston with a circle …

title: The dead aid the living in UAA anatomy lab
summary: Inside the Gross Anatomy Laboratory at the University of Alaska Anchorage, a room cooled to the mid-60s and smelling of ethanol and other preservatives, a half-dozen first-year students in the WWAMI biomedical program were quietly exploring three cadavers open to their gaze, and taking notes.

title: Man dead on Parks Highway; hit-and-run investigated
summary: Alaska State Troopers found the body of a man on the Parks Highway near Wasilla early this morning. Troopers say a caller reported the body in the southbound lane of the highway near its intersection with Vine Road in Meadow Lakes at about 3:30 a.m., and officers arriving minutes later found the man lying face-down in the road. There were no vehicles in the area when they arrived. The victim has been identified as Wasilla resident Scott L. Johnson, 22. The state medical examiner has taken his body for an autopsy. Elizabeth Ipsen, spokeswoman for the Troopers, said initial investigation indicated that Johnson may have been hitchhiking or walking along the southbound lane when he was struck by a vehicle. Friday afternoon, Ipsen said the case could be more than a hit-and-run. "It may be more …

title: Coast Guard scandal
summary: Who knew a scandal was brewing within the usually slick-operating U.S. Coast Guard in Alaska. On Monday, the Rear Adm. Arthur E. Brooks of the 17th Coast Guard District temporarily relieved Capt. H. Mark Hamilton from his duties as commander of the Coast Guard Sector Anchorage, citing alleged misconduct and loss of confidence in the officer's ability to command.Pending the results of an investigation, Brooks may recommend that Capt. Hamilton resume command or be permanently relieved, according to a statement by the agency. Final determination regarding a permanent removal or resumption of command is made by the Commandant of the Coast Guard in Washington, D.C.Capt. John S. Kenyon, assigned as the Seventeenth District Chief of Prevention, has assumed temporary command of Sector Anchorage.Ca…
